directions.evaluation: Coverage by session, activity, object, and modality; duplicate checks; train/val/test leakage checks; reproducible selection report.
directions.first_build: Episode atlas, category tags, balance report, and split builder across activities, objects, scenes, people, sessions, and missing modalities.
directions.name: Episode Taxonomy and Data Engine
directions.why_it_matters: Fine-tuning quality depends on selecting representative episodes instead of sampling randomly from a large corpus.

directions.evaluation: Versioned splits, deterministic metric scripts, task-specific confidence intervals, and model-card reporting templates.
directions.first_build: Fixed train/val/test manifests, task cards, leakage checks, metric scripts, and small reference baselines.
directions.name: Standardized Benchmark Protocol
directions.why_it_matters: Future model results become comparable across Qwen, Cosmos-style world models, policy models, and smaller task heads.

directions.evaluation: Cross-modal retrieval, missing-modality reconstruction, transfer to the 12 task heads, and held-out episode generalization.
directions.first_build: Contrastive and masked-prediction objectives over synchronized multimodal windows.
directions.name: Multimodal Representation Learning
directions.why_it_matters: Xperience-10M can train reusable encoders before committing to expensive large-model fine-tuning or pretraining.

directions.evaluation: Step boundary accuracy, transition prediction, next-step prediction, graph consistency, and long-horizon task replay.
directions.first_build: Step segmentation, transition graph, precondition/effect labels, and temporal skill graph extraction.
directions.name: Skill and Procedure Graph Mining
directions.why_it_matters: It connects egocentric perception to task structure, planning, and long-horizon embodied reasoning.

directions.evaluation: Contact F1, object micro-F1, affordance accuracy, future interaction prediction, and per-object error analysis.
directions.first_build: Contact, hand-object state, reachable object, likely tool use, and next-affordance prediction tasks.
directions.name: Human-Object Interaction and Affordance Modeling
directions.why_it_matters: The dataset can model what actions the scene affords, not only what action label is currently visible.

directions.evaluation: Map consistency, object permanence, spatial retrieval, future-state prediction, and novel-view or view-consistency probes.
directions.first_build: Persistent scene/object map prototypes built from depth, pose/SLAM, multiview video, and object cues.
directions.name: 3D/4D Scene and Object Memory
directions.why_it_matters: It moves beyond frame-level recognition toward world-state tracking, object permanence, and spatial reasoning.

directions.evaluation: QA pass rate, drift estimates, missing-view tables, corruption reports, and exclusion or degraded-mode manifests.
directions.first_build: Per-episode QA for timestamp drift, stream availability, calibration consistency, corrupted files, and missing modalities.
directions.name: Data Quality, Synchronization, and Missing-Modality Diagnostics
directions.why_it_matters: Large multimodal training fails quietly without strong data-quality gates, so QA should be a first-class artifact.

directions.evaluation: Retargeting validity, action prediction, contact consistency, imitation rollout quality, and sim-to-real assumption checks.
directions.first_build: Action-token conversion, robot-compatible targets, imitation-learning examples, and simulation transfer probes.
directions.name: Policy, Retargeting, and Simulation Transfer
directions.why_it_matters: It creates a bridge from human egocentric experience to robot policies while keeping action-space assumptions explicit.

practical_order: Build the episode taxonomy and data-quality diagnostics first.
Lock the benchmark protocol and split manifests before reporting model scores.
Add representation-learning and skill-graph objectives once enough episodes are staged.
Add affordance, 3D/4D memory, and policy-retargeting branches after labels and action targets are measurable.
additional_development_directions.public_boundary: These are proposed development tracks. They are not reported as completed held-out benchmark results.
additional_development_directions.summary: Concrete Xperience-10M project directions beyond the current minimal baselines, Qwen3-Omni LoRA plan, Cosmos/world-model branch, and long-term native pretraining goal.
additional_development_directions.title: Additional Development Directions

baseline_summary.current_use: task design, data-contract validation, case studies, and baseline comparison
baseline_summary.split: chronological single-episode split for public-sample diagnostics

directions.current_readout: The sample supports hand trajectory forecasting and contact/object probes, but it does not yet include a full body/shape model or multi-person priors.

extension_tasks.current_limit: This is a motion-energy proxy, not a SMPL/MANO body model or a generative motion prior.
extension_tasks.name: Body and Hand Motion Intensity

directions.focus: Human/hand/body motion, deformation priors, human-object interaction, affordance modeling.
directions.name: Human Modeling & Motion Understanding
next_steps: Add SMPL/SMPL-X or MANO-style body/hand parameter targets where available.
Train sequence models over multi-episode motion trajectories instead of isolated windows.
Evaluate affordance prediction on held-out objects and held-out episodes.
directions.preferred_background: Human pose/shape estimation, SMPL-style models, motion capture, or motion generation.

tasks.case_study: In the coffee-making sample, if the 20-frame window is during a pouring moment, the task asks the model to output an action such as Pour coffee or Pour milk into coffee.
tasks.current_limit: Chronological single-episode split creates unseen future action classes.
tasks.input: One 20-frame window represented by the current feature vector: video/audio/depth summaries, pose, SLAM/camera pose, motion capture, IMU, calibration, and language-derived context.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: window features -> action label builder -> classifier
tasks.why: Reads egocentric sensor state as the current human action; also provides a weak human-motion readout.

tasks.case_study: When the hand is moving toward a cup or bottle, the model predicts the future 3D hand-joint path.
tasks.current_limit: Forecasting is window-level and not yet a full sequence or policy model.
tasks.input: The current all-modality window vector at time t.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: current features -> future mocap target -> regression head
tasks.why: Directly predicts human hand motion and supports hand-object interaction modeling.

tasks.case_study: During manipulation, the hand may touch a cup, table, or bottle. The task asks whether any contact is happening.
tasks.current_limit: The public sample is degenerate for this target because one class dominates.
tasks.input: Non-contact and non-caption feature blocks, so the answer is not directly leaked from the target labels.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: feature filter -> contact target -> binary classifier
tasks.why: Targets physical interaction state, a core affordance and manipulation signal.

tasks.case_study: If the person is pouring milk into coffee, relevant objects may include milk, cup, coffee, or container-like items.
tasks.current_limit: Object labels are language-derived and sparse in one episode.
tasks.input: Non-caption feature blocks, so the model must infer objects from sensors rather than copying the caption words.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: object vocabulary -> multi-hot labels -> sigmoid heads
tasks.why: Connects egocentric activity to manipulated objects and early object-centric state.

directions.current_readout: The current suite checks cross-modal alignment and depth/video reconstruction proxies; it does not yet train a renderer or reconstruct geometry.

extension_tasks.current_limit: This checks calibrated multi-view signal, but it is still feature retrieval, not NeRF, Gaussian Splatting, or novel-view synthesis.
extension_tasks.name: Multi-View Consistency Retrieval

directions.focus: Multi-view dynamic scene reconstruction, NeRF/Gaussian Splatting, novel-view synthesis.
directions.name: 3D/4D Reconstruction & Neural Rendering
next_steps: Use calibrated multi-view video plus SLAM pose to build per-episode camera trajectories.
Add depth-supervised point clouds, TSDF, Gaussian Splatting, or NeRF baselines.
Evaluate novel-view synthesis and temporal consistency across held-out views/time.
directions.preferred_background: 3D reconstruction, neural rendering, camera calibration, and bundle adjustment.

tasks.case_study: Use motion, IMU, and camera-pose signals from a pouring moment to retrieve the matching depth/video representation for that same moment.
tasks.current_limit: Retrieval shows an alignment signal, not geometric reconstruction.
tasks.input: Query side: motion, IMU, and camera/pose features. Candidate side: depth and video features.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: modality split -> projection -> nearest-neighbor ranker
tasks.why: Tests whether synchronized modalities identify the same 4D moment, a prerequisite for reconstruction and world modeling.

tasks.case_study: Given motion, IMU, and camera-pose signals while the hand moves, predict the matching depth/video feature vector.
tasks.current_limit: Feature-vector reconstruction is not pixel, depth-map, mesh, NeRF, or Gaussian reconstruction.
tasks.input: Motion, IMU, and camera/pose features as input; depth/video features as the regression target.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: source-target split -> scaler -> regression head
tasks.why: Predicts visual/depth state from non-target sensors as a weak reconstruction/world-model objective.

tasks.case_study: Motion from a pouring moment is paired with video/depth from several windows later. The task asks the model to detect that mismatch.
tasks.current_limit: Synthetic shifts diagnose alignment but do not solve calibration or mapping.
tasks.input: A motion-side feature group and a visual/depth-side feature group, either aligned or artificially shifted.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: aligned/shifted pairs -> feature combiner -> binary classifier
tasks.why: Detects temporal desynchronization, a key data-quality gate for multimodal reconstruction and world models.

directions.current_readout: Most of the 12 tasks directly target egocentric action, task state, interaction, grounding, and alignment.

extension_tasks.current_limit: This is an action-structure probe inside one episode, not a general intent model across homes, people, or tasks.
extension_tasks.name: Action Phase Progress Estimation

directions.focus: Egocentric action and intention understanding, hand-object interaction, gaze/attention modeling, task structure modeling.
directions.name: Egocentric Vision & Interaction
next_steps: Move from single-episode chronological splits to held-out-episode splits.
Use audio together with stronger multimodal backbones for action, intent, and grounding.
Evaluate long-horizon task success prediction and action-conditioned generation.
directions.preferred_background: Video understanding, action recognition, or egocentric vision.

tasks.case_study: In the coffee-making sample, if the 20-frame window is during a pouring moment, the task asks the model to output an action such as Pour coffee or Pour milk into coffee.
tasks.current_limit: Chronological single-episode split creates unseen future action classes.
tasks.input: One 20-frame window represented by the current feature vector: video/audio/depth summaries, pose, SLAM/camera pose, motion capture, IMU, calibration, and language-derived context.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: window features -> action label builder -> classifier
tasks.why: Reads egocentric sensor state as the current human action; also provides a weak human-motion readout.

tasks.case_study: A pouring action may belong to a broader subtask such as preparing or pouring a drink. The model predicts that broader stage instead of a fine action.
tasks.current_limit: Single-episode ordering makes future subtasks hard to generalize.
tasks.input: The same all-modality window vector used by action recognition.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: window features -> subtask label builder -> classifier
tasks.why: Segments egocentric task state and provides a first proxy for symbolic world/task state.

tasks.case_study: When the demonstrator changes from preparing to pouring, the model should flag a boundary instead of a steady action window.
tasks.current_limit: Boundary class is sparse, so accuracy alone is misleading.
tasks.input: One all-modality window vector plus labels derived from action-change timestamps.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: action changes -> boundary labels -> binary classifier
tasks.why: Localizes egocentric task boundaries and diagnoses temporal state changes.

tasks.case_study: If a window shows the person preparing to pour, the target can be the action 20 frames later, such as the start of pouring.
tasks.current_limit: Unseen future labels dominate the single-episode chronological test.
tasks.input: The current all-modality window vector at time t.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: current features -> future label shift -> classifier
tasks.why: Tests action intention/task-flow prediction from egocentric context.

tasks.case_study: When the hand is moving toward a cup or bottle, the model predicts the future 3D hand-joint path.
tasks.current_limit: Forecasting is window-level and not yet a full sequence or policy model.
tasks.input: The current all-modality window vector at time t.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: current features -> future mocap target -> regression head
tasks.why: Directly predicts human hand motion and supports hand-object interaction modeling.

tasks.case_study: During manipulation, the hand may touch a cup, table, or bottle. The task asks whether any contact is happening.
tasks.current_limit: The public sample is degenerate for this target because one class dominates.
tasks.input: Non-contact and non-caption feature blocks, so the answer is not directly leaked from the target labels.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: feature filter -> contact target -> binary classifier
tasks.why: Targets physical interaction state, a core affordance and manipulation signal.

tasks.case_study: If the person is pouring milk into coffee, relevant objects may include milk, cup, coffee, or container-like items.
tasks.current_limit: Object labels are language-derived and sparse in one episode.
tasks.input: Non-caption feature blocks, so the model must infer objects from sensors rather than copying the caption words.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: object vocabulary -> multi-hot labels -> sigmoid heads
tasks.why: Connects egocentric activity to manipulated objects and early object-centric state.

tasks.case_study: A query like Pour milk into coffee should rank the windows from the actual pouring moment higher than unrelated windows.
tasks.current_limit: Bag-of-objects language features are too weak for rich grounding.
tasks.input: Caption/object/interaction query features and a set of candidate sensor-window features.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: query features -> candidate index -> cosine ranker
tasks.why: Grounds language annotation into egocentric sensor time and task state.

tasks.case_study: Use motion, IMU, and camera-pose signals from a pouring moment to retrieve the matching depth/video representation for that same moment.
tasks.current_limit: Retrieval shows an alignment signal, not geometric reconstruction.
tasks.input: Query side: motion, IMU, and camera/pose features. Candidate side: depth and video features.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: modality split -> projection -> nearest-neighbor ranker
tasks.why: Tests whether synchronized modalities identify the same 4D moment, a prerequisite for reconstruction and world modeling.

tasks.case_study: If window A shows reaching and window B shows pouring, the model should distinguish A then B from B then A.
tasks.current_limit: Only local adjacent ordering, not long-horizon causal modeling.
tasks.input: A pair of adjacent window vectors, plus their difference vector.
tasks.input_short: two adjacent windows plus difference vector
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: pair builder -> feature combiner -> binary classifier
tasks.why: Checks whether features encode local time direction and task progression.

tasks.case_study: Motion from a pouring moment is paired with video/depth from several windows later. The task asks the model to detect that mismatch.
tasks.current_limit: Synthetic shifts diagnose alignment but do not solve calibration or mapping.
tasks.input: A motion-side feature group and a visual/depth-side feature group, either aligned or artificially shifted.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: aligned/shifted pairs -> feature combiner -> binary classifier
tasks.why: Detects temporal desynchronization, a key data-quality gate for multimodal reconstruction and world models.

directions.current_readout: The current tasks probe temporal structure, object relevance, cross-modal retrieval, and modality prediction, but they do not yet build persistent maps or scene graphs.

extension_tasks.current_limit: This is a compact world-model proxy; it does not build a persistent map, scene graph, or object permanence model.
extension_tasks.name: Short-Horizon Ego-Motion Forecasting

directions.focus: Long-term consistent 3D/4D scene mapping, scene graphs, object- and space-centric representations, spatial reasoning.
directions.name: Scene Reconstruction & World Modeling
next_steps: Convert windows into persistent object/scene-state nodes with timestamps and camera poses.
Add map consistency, object permanence, and spatial relation prediction tasks.
Train held-out-episode world models that predict future observations and task state.
directions.preferred_background: Large-scale mapping, semantic reconstruction, or agent world models.

tasks.case_study: A pouring action may belong to a broader subtask such as preparing or pouring a drink. The model predicts that broader stage instead of a fine action.
tasks.current_limit: Single-episode ordering makes future subtasks hard to generalize.
tasks.input: The same all-modality window vector used by action recognition.

execution_order.action: Stage at least 32 valid Xperience-10M episodes with held-out episode split.
execution_order.name: Data gate

execution_order.action: Run Qwen3-Omni action/subtask error analysis and targeted reruns to improve the verified diagnostic baseline.
execution_order.name: First held-out baseline

execution_order.action: Run 3-8 episode dry runs for Qwen3-Omni prompt/LoRA, Cosmos 3 preprocessing, and one policy candidate.
execution_order.name: Model-selection dry run

execution_order.action: Promote Cosmos 3 if future-window/action-conditioned preprocessing fits storage and compute.
execution_order.name: World-model branch

execution_order.action: Promote OpenVLA/openpi/GR00T after action target conversion and retargeting artifacts are traceable.
execution_order.name: Policy branch

execution_order.action: Publish branch results only with real manifests, predictions, metrics, and qualitative examples.
execution_order.name: Publishing threshold

execution_order.action: Start a from-scratch Xperience Embodied Foundation Model only after smaller scaling stages, full-corpus storage, multi-node compute, and held-out evaluation protocols are in place.
execution_order.name: Xperience-native pretraining

model_families.best_role: First selected-episode multimodal LoRA pilot and structured task predictor.
model_families.entry_condition: Selected episodes prepared with held-out episode split.
xperience10m_fit: RGB/fisheye video, embedded audio, and language prompts can enter directly.
Depth, pose/SLAM, mocap, contacts, and IMU enter through the existing sensor bridge.
Matches current task outputs: labels, structured JSON, captions, and short decisions.

model_families.best_role: Embodied world modeling, action generation, future-window prediction, and synthetic-data expansion.
model_families.entry_condition: Multi-episode data plus enough storage/compute for generated or latent video-state outputs.
xperience10m_fit: Uses video streams as visual state.
Uses pose/SLAM, depth, mocap, IMU, and language as physical-world conditioning signals.
Better aligned with prediction/generation objectives than simple label classification.

model_families.best_role: Humanoid action understanding, retargeting, contact/action prediction, and embodied skill transfer.
model_families.entry_condition: Retargeting artifact and action-space definition exist.
xperience10m_fit: Hand/body mocap and contact cues can be retargeted into humanoid state/action targets.
Egocentric video plus human motion can support affordance and interaction tasks.

model_families.best_role: Open robot-policy baseline after observations and action labels are converted into a VLA format.
model_families.entry_condition: Window-to-action-token conversion is implemented and checked.
xperience10m_fit: Good candidate when each window is expressed as visual observation, instruction/context, and action token.
Requires an explicit action target; current human egocentric labels are not robot controls by default.

model_families.best_role: Action-chunking, policy fine-tuning, and embodiment-transfer experiments.
model_families.entry_condition: Action target and train/eval protocol exist for at least 64 episodes.
xperience10m_fit: Useful once hand trajectories, contacts, or retargeted body motion are converted into policy targets.
Better for policy branch than for current structured task JSON outputs.

model_families.best_role: Qualitative reasoning reference, annotation helper, and external comparison when API access exists.
model_families.entry_condition: API/access exists and outputs are logged separately from trainable model metrics.
xperience10m_fit: Can help reason over egocentric scenes and task descriptions.
Not a local fine-tune target for this repo.

model_families.best_role: Cheaper policy baselines for observation-to-action experiments.
model_families.entry_condition: Action labels and baseline protocol exist.
xperience10m_fit: Less directly omni-modal than Qwen3-Omni or Cosmos 3.

model_families.best_role: Domain model over synchronized embodied experience.
model_families.entry_condition: Full-corpus data path, PB-scale storage, multi-node compute, and positive smaller-run scaling evidence.
model_families.openness: future project-specific model if full-corpus access and compute exist
xperience10m_fit: Uses the full aligned modality stack rather than treating sensors as auxiliary metadata.
Targets temporal embodied representation learning across perception, motion, geometry, audio, and language.
Can become the shared pretraining backbone for Qwen-style instruction tasks, Cosmos-style world modeling, and policy/action branches.

source_links.label: Xperience Embodied Foundation Model pretraining plan

omni_plan.first_pilot: 32 held-out-episode pilot after valid episodes are prepared

phases.entry_condition: One public Xperience-10M sample episode is available.
phases.name: Public-Sample Task Lab
phases.reader_takeaway: The public sample supports task design, feature contracts, walkthroughs, and baseline comparisons.

phases.entry_condition: Gated dataset availability and enough storage for selected episodes.
phases.name: Multi-Episode Data Preparation
phases.reader_takeaway: The first selected split is available for Qwen3-Omni diagnostics, with train/test separation at the episode level.

phases.entry_condition: Selected episodes are prepared locally with no train/test episode leakage.
phases.name: Qwen3-Omni LoRA Final Diagnostic Result
phases.reader_takeaway: The final omni-model diagnostic result establishes the full held-out training/validation/evaluation loop and meets the strict-JSON target, but weak action/subtask metrics make it a diagnostic baseline.

deliverables: neural MLP baselines for JSON-supported labels
explicit unsupported markers for raw-feature-only tasks
phases.entry_condition: Derived Qwen JSONL export for the selected 96/16/16 split.
phases.name: 128-Episode Same-Split Simple/NN Baselines
phases.reader_takeaway: The simple and neural baseline framing is now aligned to the selected 128-episode setup; trajectory, retrieval, reconstruction, and misalignment variants still need raw 128 feature blocks for exact feature-level reproduction.

deliverables: comparison to the final verified Qwen baseline
phases.entry_condition: The final diagnostic package meets strict JSON validity but has weak action/subtask held-out quality.
phases.name: Action/Subtask Error-Analysis Pass
phases.reader_takeaway: The next pass should improve action/subtask quality before larger model-quality claims.

phases.entry_condition: The selected episodes are prepared or a 3-8 episode dry run is available for preprocessing checks.
phases.name: Foundation-Model Selection Matrix
phases.reader_takeaway: Qwen3-Omni remains the first trainable held-out pilot; Cosmos 3 is the first world-model branch; VLA/policy models wait for explicit action targets.

phases.entry_condition: The selected-episode pilot trains and evaluates cleanly.
phases.name: 64-128 Episode Robustness Run
phases.reader_takeaway: The robustness run tests whether the pilot conclusions survive broader sessions and missing modalities.

deliverables: Cosmos 3 future-window or action-conditioned world-model probe
phases.entry_condition: Enough multi-episode data, compute budget, and model-specific action/world-state targets.
phases.name: Cosmos 3 and Policy-Model Extensions
phases.reader_takeaway: The long-term direction is richer multimodal representation learning for embodied-AI reasoning, with model branches chosen by task fit rather than by a single default backbone.

phases.entry_condition: Full-corpus access, PB-scale storage path, high-throughput data loading, multi-node compute, and positive scaling evidence from smaller multi-episode runs.
phases.name: Xperience Embodied Foundation Model Pretraining
phases.reader_takeaway: The final research direction is a domain-specific embodied foundation model trained directly on Xperience-10M, after smaller pilots justify the cost and infrastructure.

scale_up.access_status: The gated Xperience-10M dataset is available for selected multi-episode pilot preparation.

scope.warning: These walkthroughs explain task contracts on one public sample episode; cross-episode performance requires held-out episodes.

tasks.case_study: In the coffee-making sample, if the 20-frame window is during a pouring moment, the task asks the model to output an action such as Pour coffee or Pour milk into coffee.
tasks.current_limit: Chronological single-episode split creates unseen future action classes.
tasks.input: One 20-frame window represented by the current feature vector: video/audio/depth summaries, pose, SLAM/camera pose, motion capture, IMU, calibration, and language-derived context.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: window features -> action label builder -> classifier
tasks.why: Reads egocentric sensor state as the current human action; also provides a weak human-motion readout.

tasks.case_study: A pouring action may belong to a broader subtask such as preparing or pouring a drink. The model predicts that broader stage instead of a fine action.
tasks.current_limit: Single-episode ordering makes future subtasks hard to generalize.
tasks.input: The same all-modality window vector used by action recognition.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: window features -> subtask label builder -> classifier
tasks.why: Segments egocentric task state and provides a first proxy for symbolic world/task state.

tasks.case_study: When the demonstrator changes from preparing to pouring, the model should flag a boundary instead of a steady action window.
tasks.current_limit: Boundary class is sparse, so accuracy alone is misleading.
tasks.input: One all-modality window vector plus labels derived from action-change timestamps.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: action changes -> boundary labels -> binary classifier
tasks.why: Localizes egocentric task boundaries and diagnoses temporal state changes.

tasks.case_study: If a window shows the person preparing to pour, the target can be the action 20 frames later, such as the start of pouring.
tasks.current_limit: Unseen future labels dominate the single-episode chronological test.
tasks.input: The current all-modality window vector at time t.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: current features -> future label shift -> classifier
tasks.why: Tests action intention/task-flow prediction from egocentric context.

tasks.case_study: When the hand is moving toward a cup or bottle, the model predicts the future 3D hand-joint path.
tasks.current_limit: Forecasting is window-level and not yet a full sequence or policy model.
tasks.input: The current all-modality window vector at time t.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: current features -> future mocap target -> regression head
tasks.why: Directly predicts human hand motion and supports hand-object interaction modeling.

tasks.case_study: During manipulation, the hand may touch a cup, table, or bottle. The task asks whether any contact is happening.
tasks.current_limit: The public sample is degenerate for this target because one class dominates.
tasks.input: Non-contact and non-caption feature blocks, so the answer is not directly leaked from the target labels.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: feature filter -> contact target -> binary classifier
tasks.why: Targets physical interaction state, a core affordance and manipulation signal.

tasks.case_study: If the person is pouring milk into coffee, relevant objects may include milk, cup, coffee, or container-like items.
tasks.current_limit: Object labels are language-derived and sparse in one episode.
tasks.input: Non-caption feature blocks, so the model must infer objects from sensors rather than copying the caption words.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: object vocabulary -> multi-hot labels -> sigmoid heads
tasks.why: Connects egocentric activity to manipulated objects and early object-centric state.

tasks.case_study: A query like Pour milk into coffee should rank the windows from the actual pouring moment higher than unrelated windows.
tasks.current_limit: Bag-of-objects language features are too weak for rich grounding.
tasks.input: Caption/object/interaction query features and a set of candidate sensor-window features.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: query features -> candidate index -> cosine ranker
tasks.why: Grounds language annotation into egocentric sensor time and task state.

tasks.case_study: Use motion, IMU, and camera-pose signals from a pouring moment to retrieve the matching depth/video representation for that same moment.
tasks.current_limit: Retrieval shows an alignment signal, not geometric reconstruction.
tasks.input: Query side: motion, IMU, and camera/pose features. Candidate side: depth and video features.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: modality split -> projection -> nearest-neighbor ranker
tasks.why: Tests whether synchronized modalities identify the same 4D moment, a prerequisite for reconstruction and world modeling.

tasks.case_study: Given motion, IMU, and camera-pose signals while the hand moves, predict the matching depth/video feature vector.
tasks.current_limit: Feature-vector reconstruction is not pixel, depth-map, mesh, NeRF, or Gaussian reconstruction.
tasks.input: Motion, IMU, and camera/pose features as input; depth/video features as the regression target.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: source-target split -> scaler -> regression head
tasks.why: Predicts visual/depth state from non-target sensors as a weak reconstruction/world-model objective.

tasks.case_study: If window A shows reaching and window B shows pouring, the model should distinguish A then B from B then A.
tasks.current_limit: Only local adjacent ordering, not long-horizon causal modeling.
tasks.input: A pair of adjacent window vectors, plus their difference vector.
tasks.input_short: two adjacent windows plus difference vector
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: pair builder -> feature combiner -> binary classifier
tasks.why: Checks whether features encode local time direction and task progression.

tasks.case_study: Motion from a pouring moment is paired with video/depth from several windows later. The task asks the model to detect that mismatch.
tasks.current_limit: Synthetic shifts diagnose alignment but do not solve calibration or mapping.
tasks.input: A motion-side feature group and a visual/depth-side feature group, either aligned or artificially shifted.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: aligned/shifted pairs -> feature combiner -> binary classifier
tasks.why: Detects temporal desynchronization, a key data-quality gate for multimodal reconstruction and world models.
